skill.name: parallel-execution
skill.description: Guidance for coordinating independent AI subagents in parallel with Task and TaskOutput workflows.
skill.summary: Run independent AI subagent tasks in parallel and synthesize their results.
skill.author: CloudAI-X

security_audit.summary: Static external-command, sensitive-data, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance detections were false positives caused by Markdown examples and ordinary prose. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found. The remaining risk is low because parallel subagents can conflict if users assign overlapping files.

low_findings.title: Parallel Task File Conflict Risk
low_findings.description: The skill warns users to avoid parallel tasks that modify the same files. This is an operational safety risk, not malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly documents conflict risk when tasks share files. The guidance mitigates the issue, so severity remains low.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed External Command Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: Static Ruby or shell backtick detections are false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline formatting for Task and TaskOutput examples, not executable commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown documentation and contains no shell execution syntax outside prose examples. The static labels do not match executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Sensitive Data Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: The Windows SAM database detection is a false positive. The flagged line contains the word CRITICAL in guidance about batching Task calls, with no credential or system database access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 48 is ordinary documentation text and does not reference Windows SAM data. No sensitive file access appears in the reviewed skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography detections are false positives. The flagged lines use words like description, Task, auth, or password reset in examples, not cryptographic algorithms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are placeholders and workflow examples. I found no MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Detections
dangerous_patterns.description: The reconnaissance detections are false positives. The flagged lines show TaskOutput identifiers and a heading about when not to parallelize work, not system inspection commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain documentation examples and no host, network, process, or filesystem reconnaissance command. No semantic reconnaissance intent is present.

content.value_statement: Complex work slows down when independent tasks run one after another. This skill shows how Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can coordinate parallel subagents.
actual_capabilities: Explains when tasks are independent enough for parallel execution.
Provides prompt structure for role-specific subagents.
Shows how to launch multiple Task calls with run_in_background.
Describes TaskOutput retrieval and result synthesis.
Covers task-based, directory-based, and perspective-based parallelization.
Lists conflict cases where sequential work is safer.
limitations: Does not provide executable automation scripts or tool wrappers.
Requires an environment that supports Task and TaskOutput style tools.
Cannot prevent conflicts if users assign overlapping files.
Best suited for independent work, not tightly ordered workflows.

use_cases.title: Parallel Feature Delivery
use_cases.description: Split independent feature tasks across subagents, then merge the completed work into one implementation plan.

use_cases.title: Multi-Angle Code Review
use_cases.description: Run security, performance, testing, and architecture reviews at the same time for faster feedback.

use_cases.title: Large Repository Exploration
use_cases.description: Assign separate directories or modules to subagents and combine their findings into a concise summary.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Parallel Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this request and identify which parts can run independently. List tasks that can be parallelized and tasks that must stay sequential.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Subagent Prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Create role-specific prompts for each independent task. Include objective, relevant files, expected output, and conflict boundaries for each subagent.

prompt_templates.title: Run Parallel Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Coordinate parallel subagents for security, performance, test coverage, and architecture review. Retrieve results and synthesize one prioritized report.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Parallel Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this feature into independent implementation tracks. Define subagent responsibilities, expected deliverables, merge order, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Review a service for security, performance, and missing tests.
output_examples.output: The skill would propose three independent review subagents, collect their findings, remove duplicates, and present one prioritized action list.

output_examples.input: Implement login, registration, password reset, and integration tests.
output_examples.output: The skill would split the work into independent tracks, warn about shared authentication files, and define a final synthesis step.

output_examples.input: Explore separate repository directories before planning a migration.
output_examples.output: The skill would assign each directory to a subagent and combine module summaries into migration risks and next steps.

best_practices: Launch all parallel Task calls in one assistant message.
Assign each subagent clear files, goals, and output expectations.
Reserve a final synthesis step to resolve conflicts and prioritize results.
anti_patterns: Parallelizing tasks where one task depends on another result.
Assigning multiple subagents to edit the same files at the same time.
Skipping result synthesis after collecting independent outputs.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure independent AI tasks so they can run in parallel and be synthesized afterward.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: When should I avoid parallel execution?
faq.answer: Avoid it when tasks depend on each other, order matters, or multiple agents need the same files.

faq.question: Can it be used for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a pattern for parallel reviews from security, performance, testing, and architecture perspectives.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What is the main safety concern?
faq.answer: Parallel subagents can create conflicting results if they work on overlapping files or dependent tasks.
